findings.location: n/a - page discusses a sweepstakes/contest prize, not a deposit account bonus
findings.reasoning: R-04 governs advertisements that state a bonus offered by an institution in connection with an advertised deposit/banking product (e.g., checking or savings account bonus), triggering disclosure of APY, time requirements, minimum balances, etc. This page is a promotional sweepstakes ('#RefundGlowUp' Contest) offering cash prizes ($10,000 grand prize, $5,000 first prizes) to entrants who post social media content about how they would use their tax refund. This is not a bank deposit account bonus tied to opening or maintaining an account with a minimum balance or time requirement - it is a marketing contest/sweepstakes unrelated to any deposit account terms. The explicit language 'NO PURCHASE, PAYMENT OR FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE OF ANY KIND IS NECESSARY TO ENTER OR WIN' underscores this is not a financial account bonus. There is no mention of any bank account, checking/savings product, APY, or account-opening requirement anywhere on the page. The strongest counter-reading would be that any 'bonus' or cash prize offered by a financial institution triggers R-04 regardless of context, but the rule text specifically ties to bonuses on 'the advertised product' (an account) with balance/time requirements - concepts inapplicable to a random-draw sweepstakes with no account requirement. Therefore the trigger condition is not met and the rule is not applicable.

Rule R-04 concerns advertisements that state a bonus on a deposit account and therefore would require APY and related deposit-account bonus disclosures. This page promotes a social media contest with cash prizes and general TurboTax tax-filing services. The only monetary amounts referenced are contest prizes (e.g., “$10,000 cash,” “$5,000 cash”) and an “extra $10,000 refund to spend,” which refer to contest winnings and tax refunds, not to any bank account bonus. There is no mention of an account-opening bonus, “bonus checking,” or any deposit product bonus. Therefore, the rule’s trigger condition is not met and the rule is not applicable to this page.

Rule R-04 concerns advertisements stating a deposit account bonus, requiring APY and related bonus timing/balance disclosures. This page promotes a social media contest with a chance to win cash and contains no deposit account offer, no APY, no minimum balance, and no account-opening language. Links like “Credit Karma Money” or “Bonus tax calculator” are navigational and not bonus account advertisements. Therefore the rule’s trigger is not met.
